identity.canonical_title: CANTOR PAIRING CASCADE FUNCTION Canonical Statutory Formula

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 910] CANTOR PAIRING CASCADE FUNCTION Canonical Statutory Formula: $Cantor(x,y) := (1 \ll (x+y)) + (1 \ll y) - 1$ Formal Technical Dissertation: A bitwise implementation of the generalized Cantor pairing function. It provides a bijective mapping of two integers into a single unique integer, recursively applied to build the address tree structure [7]. Source: [7]
theory.technical_interpretation: CANTOR PAIRING CASCADE FUNCTION Canonical Statutory Formula is an algebraic definition, written Cantor(x,y) := (1 ll (x+y)) + (1 ll y) - 1. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from C, a, n, o, r, x. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
